facts.text: In 1681, William Cooper built a home near the Cooper River where it meets the Delaware, naming the tract Pyne Point.
Camden hosts the Walt Whitman House, the cultural arts center, and the Rutgers–Camden Center for the Arts, echoing its literary legacy.
The Walter Rand Transportation Center, opened in 1989, sits on Martin Luther King Boulevard and Broadway, linking PATCO, NJ Transit, and Greyhound.
Rutgers University–Camden began as the South Jersey Law School in 1926, while Cooper Medical School of Rowan opened there in 2012.
The Benjamin Franklin Bridge, connecting New Jersey and Pennsylvania, opened in 1926 and was renamed in 1956.
The Victor Talking Machine Company, founded in 1894, perfected the phonograph in Camden and manufactured it there for over thirty years.
